_meta.purpose: WNS configuration: trigger thresholds + distance-decay budgets per NL layer.
_meta.pattern: Every NL layer fires every-N events at its own scope. Lower layers fire often; higher layers accumulate from wider scope and fire less often. Mirrors the WMS memory-config.json pattern.
_meta.tunable: All values are playtest-tuned. Adjust the JSON and restart — no code changes.

triggers._note: Per-layer firing thresholds. NL_N fires once per N events at the address scope.

nl2.rationale: Locality-scale. Five NL1 mentions at one locality is a reasonable threshold for the locality narrative to update — captures conversational turnover without firing on every line.

nl3.rationale: District-scale. Five NL2 fragments aggregated under a district means at least 2 distinct localities have stuff happening, enough to warrant district-scale weaving.

nl4.rationale: Region-scale. Eight NL3 fragments span multiple districts — region arcs need cross-district pattern density before they're worth committing to.

nl5.rationale: Province-scale. Twelve NL4 fragments span multiple regions — province tides need real momentum.

nl6.rationale: Nation-scale. Fifteen NL5 fragments worldwide — a nation re-narrates itself when its provinces have all moved noticeably.

nl7.rationale: World-scale. Twenty NL6 fragments — civilizational pulse. Should fire rarely; most firings emit zero threads.

distance_filter._note: Shallow-going-outward depth rules. Each firing tier has a list of layers it sees in 'full detail' (verbatim narrative) and 'brief summary' (truncated). char_caps controls truncation per depth.

char_caps._note: Char-budget per row at each depth. The cascading_context module's FADING_NARRATIVE_CHAR_CAP (240) is the canonical fading cap; these are soft hints distance_filter can use to trim candidate pools.

weaver_caps._note: Output caps the runtime enforces post-LLM. Per-fragment thread cap mirrors the per-layer prompt guidance.

call_wes._note: Calibration for the call_wes self-flag (now realized as inline <WES> XML tags). The LLM emits the tags; the runtime parses + dispatches. Optional deterministic fallbacks (NOT YET WIRED, hooks documented for future).

thread_count_threshold._note: If active threads at the firing address exceed N, force-fire WES even without explicit <WES> tag.

severity_threshold._note: If WMS severity at the firing layer crosses moderate→significant, force-fire.

arc_stage_transition._note: If a thread transitions rising_action -> falling_action at this firing, force-fire (turning point WES).
